status: PRE-REGISTERED — degree distributions NOT yet computed

network.source: FlyWire Princeton release (Dorkenwald et al. 2022, Nature Methods)
network.note: Degree distributions not computed prior to pre-registration. Network statistics above from file metadata only.

layer_definitions.d1: out-degree (binary): number of distinct post-synaptic neurons each neuron contacts
layer_definitions.d2: in-degree (binary): number of distinct pre-synaptic neurons each neuron receives from
layer_definitions.d1_w: weighted out-degree: sum of syn_count across all outgoing connections
layer_definitions.d2_w: weighted in-degree: sum of syn_count across all incoming connections

prior_fpl_evidence.npm_software: r(d1,d2) = -0.31 (anti-correlated: bimodal polarity, not FPL failure)
prior_fpl_evidence.note: FPL holds in connectomes; the direction in engineering/molecular networks depends on structural regime

hypotheses.statement: FPL holds at 139k scale: r(d1_binary, d2_binary) > 0 for the full connected subgraph (neurons with both d1 > 0 and d2 > 0). Based on FPL confirmed at 302 (C. elegans), 2952 (larval Drosophila F13), and multiple small molecular networks. Pre-registered before computing any degree distributions.

Weighted FPL exceeds binary FPL: r(d1_syncount, d2_syncount) > r(d1_binary, d2_binary). Synapse count weighting should amplify hub persistence because neurons with many connections tend to also form stronger synapses. First test of weighted FPL in a connectome.

NT stratification: ACH (cholinergic) neurons have higher mean d1 than GABA neurons. Biological rationale: ACH neurons in Drosophila are primarily excitatory interneurons and projection neurons that fan out to many targets (high d1); GABA neurons are primarily inhibitory interneurons that receive convergent input for local suppression (relatively lower d1 per neuron type). Note: GLUT neurons are largely motoneurons targeting muscle, excluded from this prediction (they would have low d2 = few upstream regulators in CNS). Prediction: mean_d1(ACH) > mean_d1(GABA), two-sided t-test p < 0.05.

Optic lobe FPL: r(d1, d2) > 0 when restricted to optic lobe neurons (group starting with ME, LA, LO, AME — visual processing subsystem). The optic lobe is retinotopically regular; FPL might be weaker or absent if hub structure is an artifact of column repetition rather than true network centrality. Pre-registered direction: positive (FPL holds within optic lobe), but this is an honest test — a null or negative result would be scientifically significant.

Hub-specialist distribution: top-d1 nodes and top-d2 nodes show low overlap. In the top-1% by d1 and top-1% by d2 (approximately 1,393 neurons each), the overlap is less than 25% of either set. Rationale: connectomes with FPL can have positive r(d1,d2) even when hubs are structurally specialized. If overlap is high (>50%), d1 and d2 hubs are the same neurons; if low (<25%), they are distinct structural classes. In npm software (r=-0.31) hubs are almost completely disjoint; in C. elegans (r=+0.317) they partially overlap. FlyWire's result will characterize its hub regime.

steps: Load neurons.csv.gz: build dict root_id -> {group, nt_type, nt_type_score}
Load connections_princeton.csv.gz: compute d1, d2 (binary) and d1_w, d2_w (syn_count weighted) per root_id
Restrict to connected neurons: d1 > 0 AND d2 > 0
H1: Pearson r(d1, d2) on full connected subgraph
H2: Compare r(d1_w, d2_w) vs r(d1, d2)
H3: t-test mean d1 ACH vs GABA (neurons with nt_type_score >= 0.5)
H4: Compute r(d1, d2) restricted to group in {ME, LA, LO, AME, ME.LO, ME.LOP, LO.LOP}
H5: Compute top-1% d1 set and top-1% d2 set, measure Jaccard overlap
Report: top-20 d1 neurons, top-20 d2 neurons, brain region, NT type, syn_count

novel_aspects: Largest connectome FPL test: 139,255 neurons, 5.3M directed connections (460x C. elegans)
First weighted FPL test in any connectome (syn_count per connection)
First NT-stratified FPL test (ACH vs GABA vs GLUT hub roles)
First FPL test with both optic lobe subsystem and whole-brain analysis
Hub-specialist vs hub-generalist characterization at scale
